svg.text: Mini-Map Marker
Displaying entity locations on the map via icons and ranges
I. Mini-Map Marker Component Functions
The Mini-Map Marker Component supports displaying entities on the prefabricated Mini-Map UI Controls
through visual indicators such as icons and ranges.
Must be mounted on entities; supports players, objects, and creations.
Can be enabled/disabled through default settings or controlled through node graphs.
II. Adding Components
In the Entity or Prefab editing interface, open the Component Editing Tab
Click "Add Components" then select "Mini-map Marker" to add it
Click "Advanced Editing" to expand the Editing Tab and add/configure markers
III. Basic Information and Marker Management
Initially Effective
Drop-down menu lists mini-map markers. Use checkbox
to toggle active state; syncs to Advanced Editing tab.
Mini-Map Marker List
Enumerates all mini-map markers for entity configuration.
Marker X (ID) used as node input for activation params.
IV. Marker Parameter Configuration
Display Settings
Initially Visible to All Players
Marker visible on mini-map of all eligible players once active
Follow Object Visibility
When entity is hidden, the mini-map marker is hidden as well
Marker Style
Select Type
Icon, Range (AoE), Marker, Player's Marker, Creation Icon - each with different config parameters
V. Selection Type Details
Icons
Select Icon:
Variety of icons to choose from
Background:
None or Circle
Background Border Color:
Specific colors or logic-driven: Hostility (enemies red,
friends green, self blue), Follow Own Faction, Follow
Owner's Faction
Clickable:
Click on map to display settings info
AoE (Range)
Area Style:
Solid line or dotted line border
Color:
Specific or logic-driven colors (same options as Icons)
Area Size (m):
Displays proportionally in Mini-Map UI
Marker
Point style (not modifiable). Not clickable on world map.
Color: specific or logic-driven (same options).
Player's Marker
Preview shows creator's current icon (passed via nodes).
Not clickable on world map.
Background Border Color: specific or logic-driven colors.
Show Height Difference: entities exceeding 10m marked.
Creation Icon
Only for creations. Preview only, not editable.
Background Border Color: specific or logic-driven.
Clickable: click on map to display settings info.
Marker Name and Text Content configurable.
Display Priority: When markers overlap, higher priority number appears on top.
Show Height Difference: When enabled, entities with height differences exceeding 10m are also marked on the mini-map.
VI. Managing Mini-Map Markers Through Node Graphs
Modify Mini-Map Marker Activation Status
Batch modify marker states via input list of marker IDs
Modify Player List for Visible Mini-Map Markers
Control which players can see specific markers
Modify Player Markers on the Mini-Map
Change player-specific marker configurations
Modify Mini-Map Zoom
Adjust the zoom level of the mini-map display
Modify Player List for Tracking Mini-Map Markers
Set which players track specific markers
Query Specified Mini-Map Marker Information
Retrieve marker data and properties
VII. Tutorial: Display Quest Objectives on the Mini-Map
Feature: Create a mechanism and display its icon and location on the mini-map
Create a prefab as the target mechanism, add Mini-Map Marker component
Add a new marker in the component (multiple markers can be added for switching effects)
Configure: Initially Visible to All Players, Select Icon, Show Height Difference: Enabled
Set Marker Name (e.g. "Mechanism") and Description (e.g. "A common mechanism.")
Place the prefab in the scene and enter Test Mode to verify
VIII. Tutorial: Track Target Object via Interaction
Feature: Walk up to mechanism to enable tracking of creation icon on mini-map
Create creation prefab (e.g. Ruin Grader), add Mini-Map Marker component and new marker
Configure: Initially Visible to All Players, Select Type: "Creation Icon", Show Height Difference: Enabled
Add Collision Trigger to mechanism: Initially Effective, Effective on Characters/Objects/Creations, Zoom x3
Configure node graph: Event "When Entering Collision Trigger" -> "Modify List of Players Tracking Mini-Map Markers"
Mount node graph on mechanism prefab and save
Place creation and prefab in scene, enter Test Mode to verify tracking behavior
Note: Tracking relies on events. Use collision triggers or other event sources to activate tracking nodes.
Refer to "Collision and Interaction: Triggering an Event" for trigger configuration details.
IX. Query Node: Get Entity's Mini-Map Marker Status
Use the "Get Entity's Mini-Map Marker Status" node to query whether a specific marker is currently active,
visible, or being tracked. Useful for conditional logic in node graphs.
Tip: Logic-Driven Color Options (available across all marker types)
Hostility: Enemies in red, friends in green, self in blue
Follow Own Faction: Based on current faction colors | Follow Owner's Faction: Based on entity owner's faction colors
Related Documentation:
AI Generated, based on Craftspeople Academy. Version: Luna 7
